title: lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64: returns pointer to static mutable data without const enforcement
description: In lora_l2.h line 129, lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returns 'const uint8_t *' pointing to lora_state.eui64 (lora_l2.c line 328). The const is advisory - callers can cast it away. The EUI-64 is used as the interface's link address (lichen_l2.c copies it to iface_link_addr at line 352 to avoid this). However, any caller that mutates the returned pointer corrupts the node's identity. This is a footgun for contributors: the API looks like a getter but returning a pointer to internal state invites misuse. Consider: return by value (8 bytes) or document the aliasing hazard prominently.
